rationale: Glob semantics verified against GlobMatch (ownership.go:417-460) both by source reading and a temporary scratch test (removed after use): '.claude/workflows/**' matches nested 'ms-panel.js' and deeper paths (e.g. 'sub/deep/file.js') via the trailing-** segment recursion, does NOT match '.claude/workflowsX' or '.claude/workflowsX/file.js' (segment-exact 'workflows' vs 'workflowsX' fails filepath.Match), and does NOT bleed into '.claude/skills/**'. It also matches the bare directory '.claude/workflows' itself, per this matcher's documented dialect ('trailing /** matches the directory itself OR any descendant file') — same rule the adjacent .claude/skills/** claim already relies on, so this is consistent pre-existing behavior, not a defect. TestWorkflowOwnsClaudeWorkflows has the correct assertion shape (string/*Ownership/error, identical pattern to the sibling TestWorkflowOwnsTraceAndGolangci), correctly reuses repoRootForWorkflowManifest against the live manifest, and is NOT tautological: I removed the claim line in a /tmp copy and confirmed the test fails cleanly (owner=="", own==nil). No double-ownership: grepped all 4 domains' OWNERSHIP.yaml, only workflow claims '.claude/workflows/**'. YAML indentation (2-space, matches siblings byte-for-byte) is correct. go build ./... and go test ./internal/validate/... -run TestWorkflowOwns both pass.
findings: Note (non-blocking): '.claude/workflows/**' also matches the bare directory path '.claude/workflows' itself, per GlobMatch's documented trailing-/** dialect. This is identical to how the pre-existing '.claude/skills/**' claim already behaves, so it introduces no new inconsistency — flagged only for panel awareness, not a defect.
